verse_text: So Achsah said to him, 'Give me a blessing; since you have delivered me into a southern land, give me the water as well.' And Caleb gave her the upper springs and the lower springs, as was the desire of her heart.

pericope.title: The Efforts of Judah and Benjamin

links.context: The sons of Anak (v. 14) were known as Anakim, and they were giants in size ([[DEU.2:10]], [[DEU.2:11]]). Othniel was the nephew of Caleb and also had his spirit. Thus, he subdued the Anakim. He would also be the first to deliver Israel in the time of the Judges (Jdg 3:9), and therefore was a type of Christ. Achsah, his wife, was Caleb's daughter, and Caleb gave her the upper and lower springs of water (v. 19; see also [[JDG.1:13]]-15). She was a type of the Church with its springs of water, the divine life of Christ. Therefore, through Christ and His life in the Church, one is able to fight against the giants of the passions and to live a God-honoring manner of life.
